meta.note: Season-2 STATIC config (Regime 2). Served from ROOT like curriculum.json/problems.json — NOT in data/, so it is outside the audit hash guard. The frozen curriculum.json is never touched. Track STATE lives in data/*.json; this file is read-only content: the schedule, the DP subtopic map, the CF bands, the core-CS catalog (OS≈DBMS→CN→OOP), and the LLD/HLD spines.

blocks.name: B0 — Overflow + Resurface
blocks.content: overflow queue + last night's MISSED cards (all tracks, decay-risk first) + 1 warm-up re-solve

blocks.name: B1 — DSA new topic
blocks.content: lecture @2x + canonical Tier A under 10/10/35 (the spine)

blocks.name: Core-CS recall
blocks.content: RecallArena active-production block (priority subject) — produce, then check

blocks.name: DP-from-LeetCode
blocks.content: 1–2 hard LC DP matched to today's subtopic, or weak-DP keep-warm

blocks.name: CF-Ascent climb
blocks.content: 1 band problem + 1 ladder, strict clocks; toward 1800-1900

blocks.name: System Design
blocks.content: LLD Mon/Wed/Fri · HLD Tue/Thu · machine-code Sat → produce an artifact

blocks.name: Dinner
blocks.content: movement already covered by the morning gym

blocks.name: B5 — Durability review
blocks.content: unified Leitner shuffle (ALL tracks) + cards + day log + evidence card — never trimmed

dp_lc.note: On a DP curriculum day (Striver Days 6-8) serve a hard LC problem from that subtopic; on other days, weak-DP keep-warm. Each solve cards the state-definition + transition + the trap; the deck later asks you to re-derive the recurrence from blank.

Stocks: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock IV
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Transaction Fee
MCM / interval: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick
Squares / matrix: Count Square Submatrices with All Ones
Bitmask DP: Partition to K Equal Sum Subsets

cf_ascent.note: Above the FROZEN §3.7 ladder (1100/1200/1300) — a higher OA band. Reads live rating from /api/cf (set cf_handle in data/config.json). Unsolved → CP-pattern card (binary-search-on-answer, greedy/exchange, constructive, number theory, graph, DP).

bands.focus: Div2 A/B + easy C: constructive, greedy, math, two-pointer
Div2 C / easy D: BS-on-answer, DP, graphs, number theory
Div2 D: harder DP, combinatorics, advanced greedy/constructive

corecs.note: RecallArena core-CS. Priority OS≈DBMS → CN → OOP (week-3 compression protects this order). Produce the answer BEFORE flipping; SQL topics get a 'write the query' variant. Each produced answer mints a kind:'corecs' card → Leitner spacing. Answer format: definition → example → tradeoff.

OS: critical section / mutex vs semaphore / producer-consumer
paging / segmentation / virtual memory / demand paging
DBMS: indexing (B/B+ tree, clustered vs non-clustered) + when indexes hurt
CN: what happens when you type a URL (DNS→TCP→TLS→HTTP→render)
OOP: compile-time vs runtime polymorphism (overloading vs overriding)
association vs aggregation vs composition (is-a vs has-a)

sysd.note: The second decider. LLD-heavy (higher yield for OAs / machine-coding) + HLD fundamentals. Each session PRODUCES an artifact (class diagram / API sketch / capacity estimate / code skeleton) saved as a kind:'sysd' card → later you reproduce the design from blank. Coach runs Socratic design mocks (no full solutions).

framework: concurrency if asked (lock the spot, not the lot)

numbers.latency: memory ~100ns ≫ SSD ~100µs ≫ DC RTT ~0.5ms ≫ disk seek ~10ms ≫ cross-continent ~150ms
numbers.assumptions: read:write ~100:1, ~500B-1KB/record, 1 server ≈ a few thousand QPS

companies.note: Seed tags across all tiers (a typical campus draws all of them). Tag PROBLEMS by reusing problems.json topic/pattern fields rather than hand-tagging. Surface as a filter on the command center, not a heavyweight view.
